majors.count_method: derived from the official University of Michigan Atlas API by counting unique bachelor-level study_field values; this is an API-derived undergraduate program-field count rather than a single admissions-page sentence
majors.notes: derived from the official University of Michigan Atlas API by counting unique bachelor-level study_field values; this is an API-derived undergraduate program-field count rather than a single admissions-page sentence

admissions.testing_policy: Michigan treats SAT or ACT scores as considered if submitted; the official Common Data Set marks SAT or ACT under "Consider if Submitted," so applicants may apply without submitting scores.
admissions.gpa_policy: Michigan does not publish a minimum GPA in the source used here, but its official Common Data Set marks both academic record and academic GPA as very important in first-year admission decisions.
admissions.course_rigor: Michigan recommends a general college-preparatory program. Its official Common Data Set lists 16 required academic units, 23+ recommended units, and specifically recommends rigorous coursework such as IB, AP, A Levels, honors, advanced, accelerated, and enriched classes.
admissions.recommendations: Michigan’s official Common Data Set marks recommendations as important in first-year admissions review.
admissions.essays: Michigan’s official Common Data Set marks the application essay as important in first-year admissions review.

academics: Michigan marks both academic record and academic GPA as very important in first-year admissions review.
Michigan recommends a college-preparatory high school program with strong academic rigor.

evidence.claim: Michigan uses the Common App or Coalition Application.
evidence.source_excerpt: URL for school’s online application: apply.commonapp.org or coalitionforcollegeaccess.org

evidence.claim: Michigan treats SAT or ACT scores as considered if submitted; the official Common Data Set marks SAT or ACT under "Consider if Submitted," so applicants may apply without submitting scores.
evidence.source_excerpt: In the official Common Data Set C8A table, the SAT or ACT row is marked under “Consider if Submitted.”

evidence.claim: Michigan does not publish a minimum GPA in the source used here, but its official Common Data Set marks both academic record and academic GPA as very important in first-year admission decisions.
evidence.source_excerpt: In the official Common Data Set C7 table, both “Academic record” and “Academic GPA” are marked “Very Important.”

evidence.claim: Michigan recommends a general college-preparatory program. Its official Common Data Set lists 16 required academic units, 23+ recommended units, and specifically recommends rigorous coursework such as IB, AP, A Levels, honors, advanced, accelerated, and enriched classes.
evidence.source_excerpt: The official Common Data Set marks a general college-preparatory program as recommended and lists 16 required / 23+ recommended academic units, including IB/AP/A Levels/honors/advanced coursework.

evidence.claim: Michigan’s official Common Data Set marks recommendations as important in first-year admissions review.
evidence.source_excerpt: The official Common Data Set C7 table marks “Recommendation(s)” as Important.

evidence.claim: Michigan’s official Common Data Set marks the application essay as important in first-year admissions review.
evidence.source_excerpt: The official Common Data Set C7 table marks “Application Essay” as Important.

evidence.claim: Michigan Atlas API yields 146 unique bachelor-level study_field values.
evidence.source_excerpt: Official Atlas API responses were aggregated and deduplicated by bachelor-level study_field, producing 146 unique bachelor study fields.

Phase 3 structured upgrade completed with improved majors counting, admissions extraction, and query-ready records.
Michigan admissions pages remain bot-protected in this environment, so this record now relies on official U-M Common Data Set and official Atlas API sources.
Michigan majors count is an official-API-derived bachelor study-field count, not a single registrar sentence from the admissions site.
